title: hieracium sparsum subsp. fagarasense
intro: Hieracium sparsum subsp. fagarasense, also known as Fagaras hawkweed, is a subspecies of Hieracium, a genus of flowering plants in the dandelion family. It is a perennial plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and supporting the immune system. Traditionally, it has been prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions.
